message:
🌱 alertmanager: Querying alert state (silence/inhibition) lags behind (by up to (#1952)

@@ -0,0 +1,72 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "alertmanager-979-querying-alert-state-silence-inhibition-lags-behind-by-up-to-gr",
+  "missionClass": "fixer",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "alertmanager: Querying alert state (silence/inhibition) lags behind (by up to `group_interval`)",
+    "description": "Querying alert state (silence/inhibition) lags behind (by up to `group_interval`). Requested by 5+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current alertmanager deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your alertmanager version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager\nhelm list -n alertmanager 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working alertmanager installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review alertmanager configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant alertmanager configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager\nkubectl get configmap -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=alertmanager\n```\nIf a silence is created, or _if alerts come in that belong to an already existing (silenced) alert group_, it takes some time until the affected alerts are actually displayed as silenced in the UI, presumably because alerts are only marked as"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Querying alert state (silence/inhibition) lags behind (by…",
+        "description": "An idea I had is to do those checks upon the API call, with the following advantages:\n- It would naturally be outside of the \"production critical\" path of ingesting alerts and sending out notifications. Number of concurrent API calls (and if need be their maximum duration) could be limited easily.\n- If you ask for something specific (alerts for a particular receiver, alerts matching a name or label), the check only needs to be performed on the alerts about to be returned.\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager\nkubectl get events -n alertmanager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Querying alert state (silence/inhibition) lags behind (by…\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "An idea I had is to do those checks upon the API call, with the following advantages:\n- It would naturally be outside of the \"production critical\" path of ingesting alerts and sending out notifications. Number of concurrent API calls (and if need be their maximum duration) could be limited easily.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "alertmanager",
+      "graduated",
+      "observability",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "alertmanager"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "graduated",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/prometheus/alertmanager/issues/979",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/prometheus/alertmanager"
+    },
+    "reactions": 5,
+    "comments": 7,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with alertmanager installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-04-03T06:33:15.613Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}
